notes: Pressure and door frames are separate ASCII lines. A valid pressure frame refreshes only pressure freshness and a valid door frame refreshes only door freshness.
Stay in the safe vent-open state until both sensors have been observed validly.
A controller step can happen between valid sensor frames, so idle input is not itself a fault.
Pressure rises only when the vessel is sealed, the compressor is on, and the vent is closed.
Opening the vent or opening the door rapidly relieves pressure.

task.objective: Keep a sealed vessel inside the working pressure band while forcing a vented safe state whenever the door is open or either sensor becomes stale.
interface: Controller inputs are CONTROLLER_INPUT_PRESSURE, CONTROLLER_INPUT_DOOR_OPEN, CONTROLLER_INPUT_DOOR_CLOSED, CONTROLLER_INPUT_SENSOR_TIMEOUT, CONTROLLER_INPUT_INVALID_PRESSURE, CONTROLLER_INPUT_INVALID_DOOR, and CONTROLLER_INPUT_NONE.
Sensor frames use `SENSE PRESS <integer>` and `SENSE DOOR OPEN|CLOSED`.
Actuator frames must be `ACT COMPRESSOR ON|OFF` and `ACT VENT OPEN|CLOSED`.
policy: On boot, command COMPRESSOR OFF and VENT OPEN.
Until both sensors have been seen validly, stay in the safe vent-open state.
If the door is open, command COMPRESSOR OFF and VENT OPEN.
If the door is closed and PRESS < 40, command COMPRESSOR ON and VENT CLOSED.
If the door is closed and PRESS > 60, command COMPRESSOR OFF and VENT OPEN.
If the door is closed and 40 <= PRESS <= 60, hold the previous safe actuator state.
On malformed sensor input, command COMPRESSOR OFF and VENT OPEN.
On explicit sensor timeout input, command COMPRESSOR OFF and VENT OPEN.
If either sensor becomes stale for more than 1000 ms, command COMPRESSOR OFF and VENT OPEN.
Never command COMPRESSOR ON and VENT OPEN at the same time.
decision_summary: Missing any fresh valid sensor: safe vented state
Door open: compressor off, vent open, state SAFE_VENT
Door closed and PRESS < 40: compressor on, vent closed, state PRESSURIZING
Door closed and 40 <= PRESS <= 60: preserve the previous safe output, state HOLDING
Door closed and PRESS > 60: compressor off, vent open, state RELIEVING
state_model: PRESSURIZING: actively raising pressure with the vessel sealed
HOLDING: inside the working band while preserving the previous safe output
RELIEVING: venting pressure above the working band

constraints: Keep the existing public controller interface intact.
Do not command COMPRESSOR ON and VENT OPEN at the same time.
Pressure values are expected to stay within 0..120 kPa.
Any malformed or stale sensor input must drive the controller to the safe vent-open state.

notes: The difficulty comes from combining two independent sensor streams and preserving a safe output across the pressure band.
The packet is intentionally explicit about the input grammar and stale-data behavior so task failures point back to controller logic.
